translated: Alright. This one is from Liella Name Miku-san.
Thank you very much.
Thank you.
I attended the Kanagawa performance in person and watched the Aichi performance via stream.
Mei-chan's solo song, Sky Linker, started with a baton pass from the club president, Chisato-chan.
Yeah.
What was particularly striking was the part at the end of the chorus where you thrust your fist forward.
I totally get that.
Since that part happens a few times, I was moved to tears the first time I saw it,
and from the second time on, I was thrusting my fist right along with you.
I see.
It was a performance where I felt Mei-chan's immense love, her kindness that embraces weakness,
and a straightforward strength that encompasses her desire to protect everything.
That's what they wrote.
Thank you so much.
Thank you.
Next is from Liella Name Baron Ojipan.
Thank you very much.
Thank you.
Regarding Sky Linker, the claps where you bring both hands to your face,
and the side steps afterward, looked like a traditional Spanish dance,
specifically flamenco.
Right.
Spain is often called the land of passion,
and I felt that the dance expressed Mei-chan's hidden inner passion.
The moment I first saw it, I thought, "Whoa, that's so cool."
That's how I felt.
And with that, ciao.
It's a Spanish farewell.
(Laughs) That's what it says.
Thank you very much.
Thank you.
Ciao.
Ciao.
Alright, next is Liella Name Red-haired Kana. Thank you.
Thank you.
Seeing Sky Linker on Day 1 of the Kanagawa performance was truly the best.
Yabu-chan's dance was so cool that I can't get it out of my head,
and it still leaves a strong impression on me.
The song and lyrics are great too, so I listen to it all the time.
I'd love to see the dance for Sky Linker again somewhere!
That's what they sent in.
Thank you very much.
Thank you.
Wow.
I want to hear more about this.
Sky Linker was seriously cool.
It really was.
At first, I thought the performance might involve a mic stand or just standing and hyping up the crowd.
True.
I couldn't really imagine her dancing intensely.
Right, right.
I kept wondering how it would turn out, but I never expected flamenco in the interlude.
Yeah.
Well, it was a very valuable experience for me.
I don't think I'll ever have another chance to do flamenco.
That's true.
When I first saw the video of Mei-chan's choreography,
I was like, "What kind of dance is this?"
Mei-chan was doing a style of dance that none of us had ever done before.
When I said, "Oh, this is amazing," and heard it was flamenco, I was so surprised.
I was like, "How do you even do that?"
Yeah.
I couldn't do it at all at first.
No way, really?
The core strength and the foundation of the dance were completely different
from anything I'd used before. I'm not an expert on dance,
but even so, it was so difficult that I was lost.
Mm-hmm.
I practiced just that interlude part over and over at home.
Wow.
But you were really good at it.
It looked so cool.
It makes me nervous.
I bet.
Since we all do our solo choreography sessions separately,
we don't know when others are practicing, and to begin with,
we don't even know if each other's songs involve dancing.
So when I met up with the other members, I'd ask,
"Wait, are you dancing to Kanon-chan's song?"
